output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.

Blocking uses `exact_match_multi` for kaggle_small_uber_eats_small, uber_eats_small_yelp_small, blocking on postal_code, house_number; `semantic_similarity` for kaggle_small_yelp_small, blocking on name_norm, address_line1, city.
Performance-wise, pair completeness is 1.00 for all pairs and candidate counts range from 82 to 149812.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `rule_based` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.78, f1=0.975609756097561, f1=1.0, threshold=0.75, f1=0.9500000000000001); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was constructed to load three datasets and perform LLM-based schema matching to align the schemas of the second and third datasets to the first dataset.
Blocking was configured using a mix of StandardBlocker with exact match on postal_code and house_number, and EmbeddingBlocker with semantic similarity on name_norm, address_line1, and city.
Rule-based matching was applied with multiple string and numeric comparators with specified weights and thresholds for each dataset pair.
Data fusion was implemented using a rule-based fusion strategy with attribute fusers primarily set to longest_string for 18 attributes and union for the categories attribute.
Singleton records were excluded from the fusion output.
The fusion strategy uniformly applies the longest_string fuser to most attributes to preserve the most complete string values, while using union for the categories attribute to combine sets.
Matching behavior integrates embedding-based blocking for semantic similarity and rule-based matching with weighted comparators, supporting both exact and approximate string comparisons.
Schema matching uses an LLM-based matcher with debug enabled and processes 10 rows for matching.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation adaption.
The dominant evaluation strategy used in this run is a rule-based fusion strategy named 'rule_based_fusion_strategy_eval'.
Core restaurant attributes such as name, address lines, street, house number, city, state, postal code, country, phone numbers, website, and map URL are evaluated using a tokenized match function.
Numeric fields including latitude, longitude, rating, and rating count are evaluated using a numeric tolerance match function.
Categories and other detected list-like columns are evaluated using a set equality match function after normalization.
A helper function was added to parse source IDs from fusion source columns.
A function was added to detect common prefixes in gold IDs to assist alignment.
A function was implemented to build an evaluation view aligning fused clusters to gold IDs, preferring IDs from fusion sources and falling back to cluster IDs.
List-like columns are detected and normalized consistently on both fused and gold datasets, excluding bookkeeping columns.
Coverage diagnostics were added to report direct and mapped ID coverage against gold IDs.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 67.96%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
This run was a refinement of the existing pipeline based on evaluation feedback.
The data fusion strategy was adjusted to use voting for house_number, phone_raw, name_norm, phone_e164, city, state, postal_code, and country instead of longest_string.
The fusion strategy for rating, rating_count, latitude, and longitude was changed from longest_string to maximum.
Embedding-based blocking support was introduced alongside existing rule-based matching and string comparison support.
Singleton retention was disabled in the data fusion engine.
The fusion behavior shifted towards preferring stable canonical values via voting for most string attributes instead of simply taking the longest string.
Numeric attributes such as latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count now use maximum to select the highest value rather than longest string.
Categories continue to be fused using union.
Normalization functions were added to clean and standardize critical blocking and matching keys such as postal_code, house_number, phone_e164, and phone_raw before blocking and matching.
String columns were explicitly cast to string dtype for stability in comparators and blockers.

This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a rule-based fusion strategy named 'rule_based_fusion_strategy' that matches the fusion pipeline.

Core restaurant attributes such as name, name_norm, address_line1, address_line2, street, city, state, country, postal_code, house_number, phone_e164, phone_raw, website, and map_url were evaluated using a tokenized_match function.
Numeric fields including latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count were evaluated using a numeric_tolerance_match function.
List-like fields such as categories and any other detected list-like columns were evaluated using a set_equality_match function after normalization.
The evaluation code was refactored to improve ID alignment between fused clusters and gold test set IDs.
List-like columns were detected and normalized consistently on both fused and gold data frames to ensure reliable set equality matching.
The evaluation functions were registered explicitly for each relevant column with clear function names.
Debug output was enabled with JSON format and written to a specified debug file.
The evaluation results were printed in structured JSON format and saved to a fixed output JSON file.

This run was a refinement of the existing pipeline based on evaluation results.
The data fusion strategy was adjusted to use the 'prefer_higher_trust' fuser for eight attributes including id, source, map_url, website, house_number, phone_raw, phone_e164, and postal_code.
Attributes map_url and website were changed from using 'longest_string' to 'prefer_higher_trust' for fusion.
Attributes house_number, phone_raw, phone_e164, and postal_code were changed from using 'voting' to 'prefer_higher_trust' for fusion.
The fusion behavior shifted to prefer values from sources with higher trust for critical attributes instead of relying on longest string or voting strategies.
This shift aims to improve fusion accuracy by prioritizing more reliable source data.
Singleton retention was disabled in this iteration.
Lineage fields now include the '_id' attribute.
The pipeline continues to support embedding-based blocking, rule-based matching, and string comparison.
Helper functions were enhanced to canonicalize fused outputs, including normalization of phone numbers to E.164 format and postal codes to ZIP5 format.

This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a rule-based fusion strategy with domain-specific evaluation functions.
Core attributes such as name, address components, and contact details were evaluated using tokenized matching.
Numeric fields like latitude, longitude, rating, and rating count were evaluated using numeric tolerance matching.
List-like or multi-valued fields such as categories were evaluated using set equality matching.
ID alignment was improved by mapping fused cluster IDs to gold IDs using _fusion_sources with fallback to cluster IDs.
List-like columns were detected and normalized to ensure reliable set equality comparisons.
Evaluation functions were registered explicitly for each relevant field with appropriate matching functions.
The evaluation code was refactored into a main function for clarity and maintainability.
Debugging output and evaluation result persistence were added for better traceability.
The refinement was motivated by the need to improve evaluation accuracy and coverage as indicated by the metrics.
Aligning fused IDs to gold IDs more robustly ensures correct evaluation mapping.

This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.

The fusion strategy for six attributes (map_url, house_number, phone_raw, phone_e164, website, postal_code) was changed from 'prefer_higher_trust' to a custom resolver '_prefer_nonnull_then_higher_trust'.
Singleton retention was disabled in this iteration.
The fusion behavior shifted to prefer the first non-null and highest trust value among candidates for certain attributes instead of simply preferring the highest trust value.
This change affects attributes related to contact and location information, aiming for more robust fusion results.
The pipeline continues to use longest_string for name and address fields, maximum for numeric fields like latitude and rating, and voting for normalized name and location fields.
Normalization and cleaning functions for blocking and fusion were refined to handle missing values more robustly.
The pipeline maintains embedding-based blocking, rule-based matching, and string comparison capabilities.
The changes address weak fusion behavior observed in the last evaluation by improving how null and empty values are handled in fusion.

This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.
The fusion strategy for map_url was changed from _prefer_nonnull_then_higher_trust to _prefer_longest_nonnull.
The fusion strategy for phone_raw was changed from _prefer_nonnull_then_higher_trust to _best_phone_raw.
The fusion strategy for phone_e164 was changed from _prefer_nonnull_then_higher_trust to _best_phone_e164.
The fusion strategy for source was changed from prefer_higher_trust to union.
The fusion behavior shifted to prefer the longest non-null string for map_url instead of just the highest trust non-null value.
For phone numbers, the fusion now prefers digits-only phone numbers with plausible length and breaks ties by trust, improving phone data quality.
The source attribute fusion changed to union, allowing aggregation of all source values rather than selecting by trust.
Overall, fusion strategies moved towards more nuanced and data-quality-aware conflict resolution rather than simple trust preference.
Singleton retention was disabled in this pipeline iteration.
The pipeline continues to use embedding-based blocking, rule-based matching, and string comparison support.
